name: Alaska CBPL Electrical Administrator License Search

agency.name: Alaska Division of Corporations, Business and Professional Licensing

updateFrequency: unknown; verify against the Alaska professional license search before relying on freshness assumptions
licenseTypesIncluded: electrical administrator professional license records exposed by Alaska CBPL search paths
knownExclusions: The Electrical Administrator license is supervisory and does not itself authorize the holder to perform electrical work.
Electrician certificates of fitness are regulated by the Alaska Department of Labor, Labor Standards and Safety, and are outside this CBPL source entry.
Construction contractor registration records are tracked separately from this electrical administrator source entry.
The live search and verification endpoints may be protected by technical access controls that this project does not bypass.
rateLimitNotes: Use only official public lookup paths. Do not bypass technical controls or account-gated license-verification workflows.
publicRecordsNotes: Official lookup metadata only. Absence from this source is not proof that an electrical contractor, administrator, worker certificate, or local authorization does not exist elsewhere.
officialBulkDownloadNotes: No stable, lawful bulk export shape has been validated for this electrical administrator source.
researchNotes: The official program page states that electrical administrator licensing is required for at least one employee of a valid Alaska contractor performing NEC or NESC work, but automated lookup/download support remains blocked pending lawful source-shape validation.
verificationCaveats: This source covers Alaska CBPL Electrical Administrator records, not electrician certificates of fitness or local permits.
No matching record means no match in this source at the checked time, not proof that an electrical authorization does not exist elsewhere.
verificationNotes: No supported sync or verification adapter is exposed while official source access remains unresolved.
maintainerNotes: Keep Electrical Administrator records separate from construction contractor registrations and Department of Labor worker certificates.

researchEvidence.note: Official CBPL program page reviewed; the page states the Electrical Administrator program is staffed by CBPL and describes the supervisory license requirement for Alaska contractors performing NEC or NESC work.

blocker.summary: Official lookup/download automation has not been validated against a stable lawful source shape, and protected workflows must not be bypassed.
